growth_records.medium: MMGLC (defined minimal medium, glucose sole C-source)

up.method: derived: qGlc=mu/Yxs; Yxs=mean(7.67,9.31)=8.49 gDW/100 mmol glucose=0.0849 gDW/mmol; 0.191/0.0849=2.25

sec.method: derived: (mean yield 213.47 mmol/100 mmol glc)/100 x qGlc 2.25

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: oxidative PPP branch (fraction of glucose uptake)
mfa.note: 13C-MFA; ref strain; absolute flux maps expressed mmol/100 mmol glucose in Fig 3

growth_records.cite: Daniels et al. Transcriptomic and fluxomic changes in Streptomyces lividans producing heterologous protein. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-1040-6. [PMC6302529]
growth_records.method: reported (multi-phase linear regression fit of biomass; second exponential phase mu=0.191 h-1); td=ln2/0.191=3.63 h calculated
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth in the second exponential phase was 0.191 h-1 for the reference strain... a lower biomass yield (33%) and lower glucose uptake rate (20%) were observed for the CelA-producing strain compared to the empty-plasmid strain.
growth_records.notes: 13C-MFA study; reference (empty-plasmid pIJ486) strain. mu=0.191 h-1 reported (multi-phase regression). td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.191=3.63 h. Yields measured per 100 mmol glucose (Table 5, 2 experiments) -> Yxs=8.49 gDW/100 mmol=0.0849 gDW/mmol. qGlc=mu/Yxs=0.191/0.0849=2.25 mmol/gDW/h. Each secretion q derived as (mean measured yield per 100 mmol glucose)/100 x qGlc 2.25; underlying yields and mu are experimental, individual q values are computed (arithmetic shown per entry). Biomass C content 35.17 mmol/gDW measured by elemental analysis. Absolute intracellular flux map is in Fig 3 (image, mmol/100 mmol glucose) - only oxidative-PPP fraction and cofactor balances (Table 6) extractable as numbers.

growth_records.medium: MMGLC (defined minimal medium, glucose sole C-source)

up.method: derived: qGlc=mu/Yxs; Yxs=mean(4.96,6.99)=5.975 gDW/100 mmol=0.05975 gDW/mmol; 0.115/0.05975=1.92

sec.method: derived: (mean 12.76/100) x 1.92; pyruvate markedly elevated vs reference
measured mean(118.43,119.89); CelA ~16% of total (mean 18.7 mg/100 mmol)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: oxidative PPP branch (fraction of glucose uptake)
mfa.note: 13C-MFA; increased from 51.7% (ref) to 75.3% in CelA producer
reported shift from 48/52 to 35/75 (glycolysis/PPP) in producing strain; increased TCA-cycle flux
Table 6: production 25.2, consumption -13.6; NADPH overproduction, assumed re-oxidized via transhydrogenase
higher maintenance ATP in producing strain vs 8.5 in reference

yields.Yxs_gDW_per_100mmol_glucose: exp1 4.96 (+/-0.17), exp2 6.99 (+/-0.21); mean 5.975 (33% lower biomass yield than reference)

growth_records.cite: Daniels et al. Transcriptomic and fluxomic changes in Streptomyces lividans producing heterologous protein. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-1040-6. [PMC6302529]
growth_records.method: reported (multi-phase linear regression; second exponential phase mu=0.115 h-1, significantly lower than reference); td=ln2/0.115=6.03 h calculated
growth_records.snippet: lower biomass yield (33%; Table 5) and lower glucose uptake rate (20%) were observed for the CelA-producing strain... the flux through the oxidative part of the PPP increased from 51.7 to 75.3%... increased flux through the tricarboxylic ac
growth_records.notes: CelA-producing strain (heterologous R. marinus cellulase A). mu=0.115 h-1 reported. td=0.693/0.115=6.03 h. Yxs=mean(4.96,6.99)=5.975 gDW/100 mmol glucose=0.05975 gDW/mmol -> qGlc=0.115/0.05975=1.92 mmol/gDW/h (about 15% lower than reference's 2.25, paper states ~20% lower). Secretion q = (mean measured yield/100 mmol glc)/100 x 1.92 (arithmetic per entry). Yields and mu experimental; q values computed. Notable overflow: acetate and pyruvate strongly elevated vs reference (overflow/microaerobic signatures). 13C-MFA: oxidative PPP fraction 75.3%, higher TCA flux, NADPH overproduction (Table 6).

growth_records.cite: Wang C et al. Epsilon-poly-L-lysine: Recent Advances in Biomanufacturing and Applications. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.748976. [PMC8506220]
growth_records.snippet: Streptomyces lividans ZX7-pls, fed batch fermentation 120 h, glucose + yeast extract, heterologous pls from S. albulus NK660 -> 0.0025 g/L (Geng et al. (2014))
growth_records.notes: Value CITED in this review (Wang et al. 2021, Front Bioeng Biotechnol) FROM primary paper Geng et al. (2014); not measured in the review. Volumetric productivity derived = 0.0025 g/L / 120 h = 0 g/L/h. Medium given only qualitatively (glucose + yeast extract), not quantified -> medium_composition_stated:false. No growth rate, biomass, or substrate uptake reported here; epsilon-PL is the excreted product.

sec.units: measured qualitatively by off-gas analyser (no numeric rate given)

growth_records.cite: van Dissel, Dino et al. A novel locus for mycelial aggregation forms a gateway to improved Streptomyces cell factories. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0224-6. [PMC4391728]
growth_records.snippet: the parental strain that had a maximal growth rate of around 0.25 +/- 0.02 h-1 (doubling time of 2.7 h) under the chosen conditions
growth_records.notes: Parental S. lividans 66 grown in bioreactor on TSBS; mu_max=0.25+/-0.02/h, td=2.7 h (both reported). No numeric uptake/secretion rates; CO2 tracked by off-gas only. TSBS composition not given.

growth_records.cite: van Dissel, Dino et al. A novel locus for mycelial aggregation forms a gateway to improved Streptomyces cell factories. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0224-6. [PMC4391728]
growth_records.snippet: under the conditions in the bioreactor PM01 also grew dispersed, resulting in a growth rate of 0.36 +/- 0.06 h-1 (1.9 h doubling time)
growth_records.notes: PM01 (loose-pellet derivative) batch bioreactor on TSBS; mu=0.36+/-0.06/h, td=1.9 h reported. PM01 was generated by 70 generations in a nitrogen-limited chemostat at D=0.1/h. No uptake/secretion rates reported.

growth_records.cite: van Dissel, Dino et al. A novel locus for mycelial aggregation forms a gateway to improved Streptomyces cell factories. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0224-6. [PMC4391728]
growth_records.snippet: On TSBS medium PM02 had a maximal growth rate of around 0.41 +/- 0.09 h-1 (doubling time of 1.7 h), which was significantly faster than the parental strain
growth_records.notes: PM02 (non-pelleting) batch bioreactor on TSBS; mu_max=0.41+/-0.09/h, td=1.7 h reported (>60% faster than WT). PM02 generated by evolving PM01 for a further 30 generations in a phosphate-limited chemostat at 36 C, D=0.1/h. No uptake/secretion rates.
